content.problemStatement:
Danya has a sequence AAA of non-negative integers. Initially, AAA has length NNN.

Let's define a function f(x,A)f(x, A)f(x,A), where xxx is a positive integer ≤∣A∣\le |A|≤∣A∣, as the maximum of bitwise ANDs of all subsequences of AAA with length equal to xxx.

Kostya gives Danya MMM requests. There are three types of requests:


1  v1\;v1v: append an integer vvv to AAA
2  v2\;v2v: delete an element equal to vvv from AAA; it is guaranteed that AAA contains vvv at least once, but if there are more elements equal to vvv, you should only delete one of them
3  x3\;x3x: calculate f(x,A)f(x, A)f(x,A)
Also, Kostya knows that at any time, all elements of AAA are smaller than 2k2^k2k for a fixed kkk.

Help Danya process all of Kostya's requests.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains three space-separated integers N, M, and k. The second line contains N space-separated integers a1, a2, …, aN denoting the initial sequence A. Each of the following M lines contains two space-separated integers describing a request in the format 1 v1, 2 v2, or 3 x3.
content.outputFormat: For each request of type 3, print a single line containing one integer — the value of f(x, A).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Subsequences giving optimal answers for the requests of type 3 are, in order: (6,7); (6,7,5); (6,7,7); (7,5).

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a dynamic programming approach. We will maintain a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the maximum bitwise AND of all subsequences of length j from the first i elements. We will update this array based on the type of request. For type 1 requests, we append the new element to the sequence and update the dp array. For type 2 requests, we remove the specified element from the sequence and update the dp array. For type 3 requests, we calculate the maximum bitwise AND of all subsequences of length x from the current sequence.
solution.timeComplexity: O(M * N * k) — where M is the number of requests, N is the length of the sequence, and k is the number of bits in the integers.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * k) — where N is the length of the sequence and k is the number of bits in the integers.
